content.question: To what extent is the documented increase in educational inequality in Hungary causally attributable to the age-10/12 tracking mechanism specifically, versus other school-choice and selection mechanisms operating simultaneously in the system?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental analysis exploiting variation in local access to 6/8-year gimnázium seats across Hungarian regions or cohorts; compare achievement inequality growth in regions/periods with differential exposure to early tracking, controlling for broader school-choice availability; use synthetic-cohort or regression-discontinuity designs to isolate the 6/8-year track's marginal effect from other selection mechanisms.
